explanation: The property states that globally, if the system's start-up has been completed successfully (startUpDone), then it must be true that the user selects either 'New Patient' or 'Resume Ventilation' (newPatient | resumeVentilation) before the system transitions to self-test mode (SelfTestMode). The 'V' (Release) operator is used to enforce the 'before' condition: `A V B` means B must hold at least until A holds, and if A never holds, B must hold forever. In this case, `(newPatient | resumeVentilation) V (!SelfTestMode)` implies that (!SelfTestMode) must hold until (newPatient | resumeVentilation) is true, meaning (newPatient | resumeVentilation) must occur at or before SelfTestMode becomes true.

The property states that globally (G), if the system start-up has been completed successfully (bool_exp2), then it must be that the system does not transition to self-test mode (not bool_exp4) until the user selects 'New Patient' or 'Resume Ventilation' (bool_exp3). This is captured by the Weak Until (W) operator, implied by the Release (V) operator in this context, ensuring that the selection must occur before the self-test mode is entered.

The property states that "once the start-up has been completed successfully" (represented by bool_exp1), "the user must select 'New Patient' or 'Resume Ventilation' before the system transitions to self-test mode". This is captured by the LTL formula `bool_exp1 -> (! (bool_exp3 U bool_exp4))`, which means if start-up is done, it's not the case that the system enters self-test mode (bool_exp3) until (new patient or resume ventilation) (bool_exp4) is selected. This ensures bool_exp4 occurs at or before bool_exp3. The chosen template includes this implication as its second conjunct. The first conjunct handles the condition when bool_exp1 is false, making the overall property robust.
chosen_LTL_template: (((! bool_exp1) -> (((! ((! bool_exp1) & (X bool_exp1))) U (((! bool_exp1) & (X bool_exp1)) & (X (! (bool_exp3 U bool_exp4))))) | (G (! ((! bool_exp1) & (X bool_exp1)))))) & (bool_exp1 -> (! (bool_exp3 U bool_exp4))))

explanation: The property states that globally, once start-up has been completed successfully, it must always be the case that if the system is in self-test mode, then 'New Patient' or 'Resume Ventilation' must also be selected. This captures the 'before' requirement by ensuring that entering self-test mode is only permitted if the user selection is simultaneously or already active, thus enforcing the precedence. This LTL uses template 37 to represent a continuous implication triggered by the completion of start-up.

The property states that globally, if the system is in the 'start-up done' state, then it must be the case that either 'New Patient' or 'Resume Ventilation' has been selected, or the system is not in self-test mode. This captures the 'before' constraint by ensuring that the system cannot transition to self-test mode (i.e., 'SelfTestMode' becomes true) if 'New Patient' or 'Resume Ventilation' has not been selected yet, given that startup is complete. However, this property does not explicitly guarantee that 'New Patient' or 'Resume Ventilation' will eventually be selected, thus the 'must select' part is implicitly covered only in its relation to entering 'SelfTestMode'.

The property states that globally, if the system has completed its start-up successfully (bool_exp1 = startUpDone), then the condition that the system is not in self-test mode (bool_exp4 = !SelfTestMode) must hold until the user selects 'New Patient' or 'Resume Ventilation' (bool_exp3 = newPatient | resumeVentilation). If the selection (bool_exp3) never occurs, then the system must never transition to self-test mode (bool_exp4 must always be true). This correctly captures the requirement that the selection must occur before the system enters self-test mode, once start-up is complete.

The property states that once the start-up has been completed successfully (represented by 'startUpDone'), it must always be true that the system does not transition to self-test mode ('SelfTestMode') until the user selects 'New Patient' or 'Resume Ventilation' ('newPatient' or 'resumeVentilation'). This 'before' relationship is captured by the LTL formula `!(B U A)`, meaning 'B does not occur until A occurs'. Thus, the core of the requirement is `(startUpDone -> !(SelfTestMode U (newPatient | resumeVentilation)))`. This directly maps to the second conjunct of template 275 `(bool_exp1 -> (! (bool_exp3 U bool_exp4)))` where 'bool_exp1' is the trigger 'startUpDone', 'bool_exp3' is the event that must be avoided ('SelfTestMode') as the first argument of the 'U' operator, and 'bool_exp4' is the event that must happen first ('newPatient | resumeVentilation') as the second argument of 'U'.
chosen_LTL_template: (((! bool_exp1) -> (((! ((! bool_exp1) & (X bool_exp1))) U (((! bool_exp1) & (X bool_exp1)) & (X (! (bool_exp3 U bool_exp4))))) | (G (! ((! bool_exp1) & (X bool_exp1)))))) & (bool_exp1 -> (! (bool_exp3 U bool_exp4))))

explanation: This LTL property ensures that once the system startup is complete, it will not immediately enter Self-Test Mode unless "New Patient" or "Resume Ventilation" has been selected. The "Globally" (G) operator signifies that this condition must hold at all times. The "if-then" (->) implication states that if the startup is done (bool_exp1) AND neither "New Patient" nor "Resume Ventilation" has been selected (bool_exp2), then in the very next state (X), the system must not be in Self-Test Mode (bool_exp3). This enforces that the required selection must occur before a transition to Self-Test Mode following startup.

The property states that globally, if the system has successfully completed start-up (bool_exp1) AND it transitions into self-test mode (bool_exp2), then the user must have selected 'New Patient' or 'Resume Ventilation' (bool_exp3). This captures the ‘before’ constraint by ensuring that the system cannot be in self-test mode, once started, without the required user selection having taken place (at or before that point in time).

The LTL property states that globally, if the start-up has been successfully completed (startUpDone), then it must hold that either 'New Patient' or 'Resume Ventilation' has been selected, or the system is not in self-test mode. This can be rephrased as: if 'startUpDone' is true, and the system attempts to transition to 'SelfTestMode' (i.e., 'SelfTestMode' becomes true), then 'New Patient' or 'Resume Ventilation' must also be true at that point. This effectively enforces that the selection of 'New Patient' or 'Resume Ventilation' must occur before the system transitions to 'SelfTestMode', given that the start-up is complete. The template perfectly captures the crucial 'before' constraint.
